text.title: mcp-resource-vs-tool-vs-prompt Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 high-impact failure modes for mcp-resource-vs-tool-vs-prompt with detectors and fixes that map back to rules in 01-core-rules.xml.

antipattern.symptom: LLM calls 'docs.read' as a Tool every turn
antipattern.root-cause: Doc surface exposed as a Tool — LLM pays attention to it.
antipattern.fix: Re-classify as Resource; LLM only reads when context requests.
antipattern.detector: Token usage drops; selection accuracy on other tools improves.

antipattern.symptom: Slash command implemented as a Resource
antipattern.root-cause: /refresh-cache was a Resource — no side-effect path.
antipattern.fix: Reclassify as Tool or Prompt+Tool combo.

antipattern.symptom: Prompt template that mutates server state
antipattern.root-cause: Slash command Prompt also writes a log.
antipattern.fix: Prompts must be pure; route side-effects through a Tool.

antipattern.symptom: Agent picks Resource when it should call Tool
antipattern.fix: Name Resources as nouns, Tools as verbs; mention in description.
antipattern.detector: Eval selection on a labeled set.
